rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned in topic and constructs: both study channel buyer–supplier relationships, transaction-specific investments, and relational governance contingencies (e.g., norms/solidarity and relationship stage/extendedness). The citing paper’s stated gap and contribution (testing when specific investments shift from expropriation to bonding/opportunism effects) plausibly builds on the cited paper’s prior evidence on safeguarding TSIs via control mechanisms, so this is not an obvious incidental mention.
